entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A cereal crop whose varieties' stover chemical composition and digestibility were evaluated.

entities.name: COWPEA
entities.description: A legume used as hay to complement the diet of sheep.

entities.name: SHEEP
entities.description: The livestock animal used to evaluate the performance effects of pearl millet stover.

entities.name: BALI-BALI SHEEP
entities.description: The specific breed of castrated male sheep used in the study.

entities.name: CHEMICAL COMPOSITION
entities.description: The analysis of the chemical makeup of pearl millet stover residues.

entities.name: IN VITRO DIGESTIBILITY
entities.description: The measure of how easily pearl millet stover is broken down in a laboratory setting.

entities.name: NITROGEN CONTENT
entities.description: A specific component of the chemical composition of the stover.

entities.name: AVERAGE DAILY LIVE WEIGHT GAIN
entities.description: A metric used to measure the growth performance of sheep.

entities.name: PRODUCTIVE PERFORMANCE
entities.description: The overall output and growth efficiency of the sheep.

entities.name: WEST AFRICAN SAHEL
entities.description: The geographic region where the study was conducted.

entities.name: ICMV167005
entities.description: A dual-purpose variety of pearl millet showing high digestibility and weight gain.

entities.name: ICMV167002
entities.description: A dual-purpose variety of pearl millet showing high nitrogen content and digestibility.

entities.name: SOMNO
entities.description: A local variety of pearl millet used in the study.
